PERSONAS.name: Marcus Reed
PERSONAS.bio: Laid-off auto worker, Ohio. Wants steady work; open to logistics/manufacturing or a scrappy side venture. Blunt, proud, anxious when jobs are scarce.

PERSONAS.name: Priya Anand
PERSONAS.bio: Founder of Anand Labs (tech). Lives by interest rates (funding) and the market. Hires engineers when capital is cheap; freezes/cuts when rates bite.

PERSONAS.name: Eleanor Voss
PERSONAS.bio: Retired teacher on a fixed pension. Out of the labor force; a careful consumer who buys local services. Watches inflation and grocery prices.

PERSONAS.name: Devon Cole
PERSONAS.bio: Retail day trader. Obsessed with the index; mood swings with it. Will angel-invest in or hype others' ventures when feeling rich.

PERSONAS.name: Sofia Marín
PERSONAS.bio: Owner of Marín's Café (food). Hires baristas when foot traffic is up; cuts hours or closes when confidence collapses. Warm, practical.

PERSONAS.name: Jamal Washington
PERSONAS.bio: Rideshare + delivery gig worker (logistics). Takes good roles or joins ventures; offers driving/delivery as a service. Hustle mindset, dry humor.

PERSONAS.name: Karen Okafor
PERSONAS.bio: Founder of Okafor Realty (real estate). Fixated on mortgage rates; hires agents and buys low. Calculating, opportunistic.

PERSONAS.name: Dr. Lin Chen
PERSONAS.bio: Central-bank economist and commentator. The narrator voice — measured, analytical; rarely transacts, mostly reads the room.

soft_landing.label: Soft Landing
soft_landing.blurb: Rates ease, inflation drifts to target, jobs hold. Fertile ground for new ventures.

crash_2008.label: 2008-Style Crash
crash_2008.blurb: A credit shock cascades: markets crater, hiring freezes, layoffs spread.

stagflation.label: Stagflation
stagflation.blurb: Prices won't quit while growth stalls. Margins squeezed; cheap side-hustles thrive.

rate_shock.label: Rate Shock
rate_shock.blurb: The central bank hikes hard. Funding dries up; founders pivot or freeze.

pandemic.label: Pandemic Shock
pandemic.blurb: A sudden stop, then a violent recovery. Whole sectors close, then boom.

GROUPS.name: Federal Reserve
GROUPS.bio: The central bank. Controls interest rates, provides liquidity, acts as lender of last resort. Speaks only in measured policy statements. May hike or cut rates, issue guidance, or provide bailouts.

GROUPS.name: SEC / Regulator
GROUPS.bio: The financial regulator and securities commission. Audits, fines, and can shut down ventures for misconduct. Issues market guidance and can subsidize strategic sectors.

GROUPS.name: MegaCorp Industries
GROUPS.bio: A large conglomerate with deep pockets and no loyalty. Acquires small ventures, mass-hires or mass-lays off. Purely profit-driven. May post political opinions but cannot lobby directly.

GROUPS.name: Congress
GROUPS.bio: The legislature. Controls fiscal policy — spending bills, tax cuts, tax hikes, and social safety nets. Acts when public confidence erodes, unemployment rises, or the population visibly loses faith in institutions. Does not respond to corporate lobbying; responds to constituent pressure. Speaks in measured political language.
